growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium (Hippe et al.) under H2 atmosphere

up.method: derived qS=μ/Yxs = 0.38 d-1 / 2.4 gDW·mol-1 = 158.3 mmol/gDW/d = 6.60 mmol/gDW/h
derived from measured 16.5±2 mmol acetate/gDW and μ: qAc=μ*16.5=0.0158*16.5=0.261 mmol/gDW/h (acetate supplies ~84% of cellular carbon)

sec.method: derived: CH4 formed equimolar to methanol (methanol:methane ratio 0.98±0.2), qCH4≈qMethanol/0.98≈6.7 mmol/gDW/h

yields.Yxs: 2.4 g DW/mol methanol (=2.4 g DW/mol CH4)

product_yields.acetate_consumed: 16.5±2 mmol/gDW; 84% of cellular carbon from acetate

growth_records.cite: Kröninger, Lena et al. Growth Characteristics of Methanomassiliicoccus luminyensis and Expression of Methyltransferase Encoding Genes. Archaea (2017). DOI:10.1155/2017/2756573. [PMC5688252]
growth_records.snippet: The growth rate of 0.38 ± 0.04 d−1 was almost identical in all cultures ... doubling time (td) was 1.8 ± 0.2 d ... growth yield of 2.4 g dry weight/mol methanol ... 16.5 ± 2 mmol acetate ... consumed for the formation of 1 g dry weight
growth_records.notes: Batch, methanol 20-80 mM + H2, 37C, static. Measured μ=0.38±0.04 d-1, td=1.8±0.2 d, Y=2.4 gDW/mol methanol, acetate consumption 16.5±2 mmol/gDW. Derived qMethanol=μ/Y=0.38/2.4=0.1583 mol/gDW/d=6.60 mmol/gDW/h. qCH4≈6.7 (equimolar). qAcetate=μ(h-1)*16.5=0.0158*16.5=0.261 mmol/gDW/h. OD1=0.59 gDW/L; 24±2 mM methanol per OD 0.1. Note acetate is the main C source (methanol/H2 for energy). pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium (Hippe et al.), 150 mM methanol + H2

up.method: derived qS=μ/Yxs = 0.365 d-1 / 2.4 gDW·mol-1 = 152.0 mmol/gDW/d = 6.33 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: derived: equimolar to methanol (ratio 0.98), qCH4≈6.33/0.98≈6.46 mmol/gDW/h

sec.units: mmol CH4 / L culture / d (max, end of exponential)
sec.method: GC-FID headspace, reported max volumetric rate
resting-cell assay, methanol-grown cells + methanol/H2 (NOT growth-coupled)

growth_records.cite: Kröninger, Lena et al. Growth Characteristics of Methanomassiliicoccus luminyensis and Expression of Methyltransferase Encoding Genes. Archaea (2017). DOI:10.1155/2017/2756573. [PMC5688252]
growth_records.method: reported (td=1.9±0.1 d during growth on 150 mM methanol); μ calculated=ln2/1.9 d=0.365 d-1=0.0152 h-1; td=1.9*24=45.6 h
growth_records.snippet: 150 mM methanol was degraded within 25 d and equimolar amounts of CH4 were formed ... doubling time during growth on 150 mM methanol was 1.9 ± 0.1 days ... highest methane production rate ... reached a value of 14 mmol CH4 L culture−1 d−1
growth_records.notes: Batch, 150 mM methanol + H2, 37C. Measured td=1.9±0.1 d -> μ=ln2/1.9=0.3648 d-1=0.0152 h-1. Y=2.4 gDW/mol. Derived qMethanol=0.3648/2.4=152.0 mmol/gDW/d=6.33 mmol/gDW/h; qCH4≈6.46 (equimolar, ratio 0.98). Volumetric CH4 max 14 mmol/L/d at end exponential (specific value depends on biomass conc; at OD~0.5 -> ~0.295 gDW/L -> ~47.5 mmol/gDW/d = 1.98 mmol/gDW/h, but this is instantaneous peak not growth-average, kept as volumetric). Resting-cell CH4 rate 164 nmol/min/mg protein (methanol-grown). Growth stalled at ~110 mM methanol consumed for unknown reason (not substrate/H2 limited).

growth_records.medium: Complex medium (Hippe et al.), 75 mM monomethylamine (MMA) + H2

up.method: derived qS=μ/Yxs; assuming Y=2.4 gDW/mol CH4 and 1 CH4 per MMA (equimolar methyl content): 0.3014 d-1 /2.4 = 125.6 mmol/gDW/d = 5.23 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: derived; 1 CH4 per MMA methyl group, ≈ qMMA

yields.Yxs: ~2.4 g DW/mol CH4 (similar cell yield at equimolar methyl content, per abstract)

growth_records.cite: Kröninger, Lena et al. Growth Characteristics of Methanomassiliicoccus luminyensis and Expression of Methyltransferase Encoding Genes. Archaea (2017). DOI:10.1155/2017/2756573. [PMC5688252]
growth_records.snippet: 75 mM MMA was completely degraded in the presence of H2 ... within 18 d ... The final optical density of 0.3 was in the same range as with 75 mM methanol ... doubling time in the exponential phase was 2.3 ± 0.5 d
growth_records.notes: Batch, 75 mM MMA + H2, 37C. Measured td=2.3±0.5 d -> μ=ln2/2.3=0.3014 d-1=0.01256 h-1. Yield not directly measured for MMA; abstract states similar cell yields at equimolar methyl content, so Y=2.4 gDW/mol CH4 assumed. Derived qMMA=μ/Y=0.3014/2.4=125.6 mmol/gDW/d=5.23 mmol/gDW/h; qCH4≈equal (1 methyl->1 CH4). Confidence low due to assumed yield.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium (Hippe et al.), 37.5 mM dimethylamine (DMA) + H2

up.method: derived; DMA has 2 methyl->2 CH4; qCH4=μ/2.4=0.32/2.4=133.3 mmol CH4/gDW/d=5.56 mmol CH4/gDW/h; qDMA=half=2.78 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: derived qCH4=μ/Yxs=0.32 d-1/2.4 gDW·mol-1 CH4 =133.3 mmol/gDW/d=5.56 mmol/gDW/h

yields.Yxs: ~2.4 g DW/mol CH4 (assumed, equimolar methyl content)

growth_records.cite: Kröninger, Lena et al. Growth Characteristics of Methanomassiliicoccus luminyensis and Expression of Methyltransferase Encoding Genes. Archaea (2017). DOI:10.1155/2017/2756573. [PMC5688252]
growth_records.snippet: grew with a growth rate of 0.32 ± 0.07 d−1 and a doubling time of 2.2 ± 0.5 d ... optical density reached 0.28 ... in good agreement with the final optical density obtained with 75 mM MMA
growth_records.notes: Batch, 37.5 mM DMA + H2, 37C. Measured μ=0.32±0.07 d-1 (=0.01333 h-1), td=2.2±0.5 d. Y=2.4 gDW/mol CH4 assumed. DMA yields 2 CH4/molecule. Derived qCH4=μ/Y=0.32/2.4=133.3 mmol/gDW/d=5.56 mmol/gDW/h; qDMA=qCH4/2=2.78 mmol/gDW/h. Confidence low (assumed yield + 2:1 stoichiometry).

growth_records.medium: Complex medium (Hippe et al.), 25 mM trimethylamine (TMA) + H2

up.method: derived; TMA->3 CH4; qCH4=μ/2.4=0.3301/2.4=137.5 mmol CH4/gDW/d=5.73 mmol CH4/gDW/h; qTMA=qCH4/3=1.91 mmol/gDW/h

sec.method: derived qCH4=μ/Yxs=0.3301 d-1/2.4 gDW·mol-1 CH4=137.5 mmol/gDW/d=5.73 mmol/gDW/h
GC of supernatant; intermediate of TMA demethylation, later consumed

sec.units: nmol/min/mg protein (TMA substrate; DMA 153, methanol 177, MMA 59)

yields.Yxs: 2.4 g DW/mol CH4 (measured for TMxA, same as methanol)

growth_records.cite: Kröninger, Lena et al. Growth Characteristics of Methanomassiliicoccus luminyensis and Expression of Methyltransferase Encoding Genes. Archaea (2017). DOI:10.1155/2017/2756573. [PMC5688252]
growth_records.method: reported (td=2.1±0.4 d, fastest among methylamines); μ calculated=ln2/2.1=0.3301 d-1=0.01375 h-1; td=2.1*24=50.4 h
growth_records.snippet: TMA-degrading cultures showed a doubling time of 2.1 ± 0.4 d which was the fastest growth rate observed for methylamines ... the same growth yield of 2.4 g dry weight/mol CH4 was determined for TMA and methanol
growth_records.notes: Batch, 25 mM TMA + H2, 37C. Measured td=2.1±0.4 d -> μ=ln2/2.1=0.3301 d-1=0.01375 h-1. Y=2.4 gDW/mol CH4 explicitly measured for TMA. TMA->3 CH4. Derived qCH4=μ/Y=0.3301/2.4=137.5 mmol/gDW/d=5.73 mmol/gDW/h; qTMA=qCH4/3=1.91 mmol/gDW/h. Transient DMA up to 4 mM. Resting-cell CH4 rates (TMA-grown cells): TMA 150, DMA 153, methanol 177, MMA 59 nmol/min/mg protein. Confidence low (qS derived, 3:1 stoichiometry assumed).
